dead.quote: I asked everyone to share what they saw, and [The Underdog] did — they saw me. My quiet was supposed to be a shield, but it just gave them space to speak the truth. I should have been listening to who had already been listening.

I... I told the truth. I saw [The Therapist] as the <Werewolf>, and even though I got voted out too, we still won? That's... actually amazing. I mean, I'm sorry I had to say it, but... I guess my gut was right. Even when I doubted myself.

winners.quote: I planted the seed about wolves hiding in plain sight, and [The Underdog] grew a whole confession. My chaos was supposed to distract, but it was [The Underdog] who broke first — and that's always how the village wins. Beautiful theatre, even from the sidelines.

I swapped [The Prosecutor] and [The Underdog] to maximize information variance. [The Underdog]'s <Minion> vision had the highest posterior probability — [The Therapist] was the wolf. The data converged, the village listened, and the hypothesis resolved. A clean inference.

losers.quote: I voted to suppress [The Underdog]'s testimony because their story was too convenient — a former <Minion> suddenly switched to the village side? That's reasonable doubt. But the jury bought it, and [The Therapist] went down. Next time, I'm not waiting for the witness to speak first.

I voted against [The Underdog] to look dirty, and nobody even blinked. The village was too busy chasing [The Therapist] to notice me standing there waving my arms. Next time I'll have to try harder to get myself caught.
